Full path: /opt/dtk-26.04-DCC2602-0317/hip/bin/hipprof
Usage:
  hipprof [options] <app command line>

Options:
  -h                          - this help.
  -o <output file>            - for the output filepath[a/, a/b/, /a/b/] and filename[a, a/b, /a/b/c].
  -d <data directory>         - to change the temporary directory [/tmp].
  --output-type <type code>   - specify output trace file format (options: 0=json, 1=html, 2=pftrace default: 2).

tracing options:
  --hiptx-trace               - to enable HIPTX application code annotation trace.
  --hiptx-kernel              - to enable HIPTX kernel application code annotation trace.
  --hip-trace                 - to trace HIP, HIP copy, HIP kernel generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --libc-trace                - to trace LIBC generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --hsa-trace                 - to trace HSA, HSA copy, HSA kernel generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --cl-trace                  - to trace OpenCL, OpenCL copy, OpenCL kernel generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --omp-trace                 - to trace OpenMP, OpenMP copy, OpenMP kernel generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --rccl-trace                - to trace RCCL, RCCL kernel generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --mpi-trace                 - to trace MPI generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --barrier                   - to trace HIP barrier, OpenCL barrier, OpenMP barrier generates API execution stats and trace file.
  --kernel-stack              - export csv file [<result_pid>.stack.csv] to save the stack information of the kernel.

  --no-export                 - export trace db only.
  --stats                     - print and export statistics only without trace file.
  --show-pid                  - print and export statistics grouped by PID.
  --segment-size <size>       - the maximum number of traces in a single trace file, default 5000000, at least 5000.
  --group-stream              - to export trace time line group by stream.
  --trace-args                - tracking collection parameters (not collected by default since version 25.04.2).
  --devices <device id>       - only the OPS and COPY of the specified device[0 0,1 0,1,2] are output.
  --print-trace               - print performance analysis process data of HIP、OpenCL、RCCL、OpenMP on the terminal.
  --mask-trace <config file>  - specify APIs of HIP for analysis and reduce the amount of data to analyze.
  --libc-trace-level <level>  - limit the api level of the traced libc,default level 1. 1:track of all libc apis,0:track of common libc apis.
  --trace-off                 - to disable tracing on start.

  --db <db file name>         - export data from the existing database(s)[1.db, 2.db:3.db:4.db] specified via --db.
  --db-path <db path>         - export data from the existing database(s) in db path specified via --db-path.
  --db-merge <db file path>   - merge multiple db files and export new combined traces.
  --merge-type <type id>      - select merge type, 0 is default original time, 1 is time alignment.
  --show-trace-id             - show the trace-ids in the dbs specified by --db or --db-path .
  --set-trace-id <trace id>   - filter trace data in the databases specified by --db or --db-path.
  --index-range <start:end>   - to export partial data by index range, start and end are separated by ':'.

  --flush-interval <ms>       - auto flush memory to database when the time interval between the last flush time and the current time is greater than the flush time, default is 5000 ms.
  --buffer-size <size>        - auto flush memory to database when api record count is larger than buffer-size, default is 100000.
  --exit-cleanup              - to intercept system calls such as '_exit'.
  --follow-fork               - to track child processes to monitor their execution status.

cluster options:
  --master                    - to enable the master node server.
  --port <port num>           - to specify the communication interface.
  --node-list <ip|name[,...]> - to specify child nodes to perform performance profiling. examples:b03r4n11,b03r3n[00-02,05].
  --data-mode <mode id>       - to specify the data transmission method from the worker node to the head node.0:DB,1:RT,2:UNSET.
  --slave                     - to enable the slave node client.
  --master-address <ip|ip:port>- to specify the communication ip address of the master node, default eno1 network card.
  --netcard <card name>       - to specify the communication network card name.
  --pid-file <pid file name>  - to specify the pid filename.

leak-check options:
  --leak-check                - check host and device leak memory, output <result_pid>.leakcheck.html and <result_pid>.mem<pid>.html.
  --check-minsize <size>      - less than this size, no stack information is generated, default is 1.
  --memory-usage              - generates memory use chart webpage. output <result_pid>.mem<pid>.html.
  --db <db file name>         - export leak-check data from the existing database specified via --db.
  --time-range <start:end>    - filter leak data in the database specified by --db based on time range(Unit second).
  --record-memfreeinfo        - record memory free information, default no record.
  --show-limit <limit_num>    - limit the quantity of leak function stacks.

session options:
  --session <name>            - dynamic control of the performance analysis process or leak-check process through the session(server).
  --session-client <name>     - dynamic control of the performance analysis process or leak-check process through the session(client).
  --show-nodes                - use instruction to get cluster node information --session-client <--show-nodes>.
  --start                     - use instruction to control tracing or leak-check --session-client <--start>.
  --stop                      - use instruction to control tracing or leak-check --session-client <--stop>.
  --flush                     - use instruction to control tracing --session-client <--flush>.
  --exit                      - use instruction to control tracing --session-client <--exit>.

pmc options:
  --pmc                       - to open pmc performance profile.
  --pmc-read                  - to open pmc read focus profile.
  --pmc-write                 - to open pmc write focus profile.
  --pmc-type                  - select pmc export type 0(default) is full name of performance, 1 is simplified name, 2 is basic data, 3 is csv table.
  --pmc-off                   - to disable pmc on start.
  --kernel-name <kernel>      - filter the kernel name for profiling.
  --kernel-profile-mode <mode> - set kernel profile data collection mode. Mode 0 is default; Mode 1 (external) adds OpenCL support.

coredump analyzer options:
  --input-core <core file>    - enable core dump analyzer and set input core file.

code object analyzer options:
  --codeobj-analyze <ELF file> - analyze register pressure of kernel functions in ELF files.
